title: Agent Run Lifecycle
description: Core agent run loop from invocation through turn execution to final output, including max-turns guard and early exit conditions.
key_actors: Runner (public facade: run, run_sync, run_streamed class methods)
Agent (starting_agent / current_agent; can change on handoff)
RunContextWrapper (wraps user context + usage + tool-approval state)
RunConfig (global settings: max_turns, guardrails, model, session, tracing)
run_single_turn() (LLM call + tool execution; returns SingleStepResult)
entry_points: Runner.run(starting_agent, input, *, context, max_turns=DEFAULT_MAX_TURNS, hooks, run_config, error_handlers, previous_response_id, conversation_id, session) -> RunResult
activity_flow: [YES - resumed] Restore: current_turn, original_input, generated_items, context_wrapper from RunState; apply saved conversation_id/previous_response_id
[NO  - fresh]  Cast input to str|list; validate session vs. server-conversation exclusivity; call prepare_input_with_session() to merge session history
Resolve reasoning_item_id_policy and server_conversation_tracker (OpenAIServerConversationTracker or None)
Initialize: current_turn=0, current_agent=starting_agent, generated_items=[], model_responses=[], session_items=[]
Create task_span; optionally pre-save session input items
Split guardrails into sequential (run_in_parallel=False) and parallel (run_in_parallel=True)
Run sequential guardrails first (blocking). If InputGuardrailTripwireTriggered → persist session, RAISE
[YES] asyncio.gather(run_input_guardrails(parallel), model_task); if tripwire → cancel model_task, persist session, RAISE
SUBSEQUENT TURNS (>1): turn_result = await run_single_turn(...) directly
Attach usage delta to turn_span; finish turn_span
[YES] call handler → validate output → synthesize message item → run output guardrails → build RunResult → RETURN (graceful recovery)
Build RunResult(input, new_items, raw_responses, final_output, last_agent, guardrail_results)
RETURN RunResult (paused; caller resumes by passing RunState back)
[NextStepRunAgain]  (tool calls were executed; loop for next LLM call)
CONTINUE loop (same agent, appended tool results in generated_items)
BaseException caught: attach RunErrorDetails to exc.run_data; re-raise
Specific catchable exceptions: MaxTurnsExceeded, InputGuardrailTripwireTriggered, OutputGuardrailTripwireTriggered, ModelBehaviorError, ToolTimeoutError, UserError

next_step_types.NextStepFinalOutput: LLM produced text/typed output with no pending tool calls → terminal
next_step_types.NextStepHandoff: LLM called a handoff tool → switch agent, continue loop
next_step_types.NextStepRunAgain: LLM made function/tool calls → execute tools, append results, loop
next_step_types.NextStepInterruption: Tool requires human approval (approval flow) → pause, return RunState checkpoint

guardrail_timing.input_guardrails: Turn 0 only; sequential run before model call; parallel run concurrently with model call
guardrail_timing.output_guardrails: After every NextStepFinalOutput (including max_turns handler output)

max_turns_behavior.with_handler: error_handlers['max_turns'](RunErrorHandlerInput) -> RunErrorHandlerResult; synthesize message item; run output guardrails; return RunResult gracefully

key_data_objects.RunConfig: model, model_provider, model_settings, session_settings, input_guardrails, output_guardrails, handoff_input_filter, nest_handoff_history, call_model_input_filter, reasoning_item_id_policy, tracing_disabled, workflow_name, trace_id, group_id, tool_error_formatter
key_data_objects.RunContextWrapper: context: TContext, usage: Usage, turn_input: list, _approvals: dict[str, _ApprovalRecord], tool_input
key_data_objects.RunResult: input, new_items, raw_responses, final_output, _last_agent, input_guardrail_results, output_guardrail_results, interruptions, context_wrapper, max_turns
key_data_objects.RunState: checkpoint for pause/resume; holds current_agent, current_turn, generated_items, model_responses, session_items, _current_step (NextStep)

important_notes: Runner is a stateless class-method facade; AgentRunner holds all logic.
Only the FIRST agent's input guardrails run (not on handoff agents).
A 'turn' = one LLM invocation; tool calls within that invocation do NOT count as extra turns.
Handoffs change current_agent but do NOT reset current_turn counter.
run_single_turn() handles: LLM call, tool execution, approval checks, hooks, and returns SingleStepResult with next_step.
Interruption (NextStepInterruption) returns RunResult with pending approvals; caller resumes by passing the RunState back as input.
Session persistence and server-managed conversation (conversation_id / previous_response_id) are mutually exclusive per run.
call_model_input_filter in RunConfig can trim/edit the input list immediately before each LLM call.
